title: Error when specializing a class by the same class
body:
Hello!

Attempting to specialize a class with the same class that is also specialized results in an error.

The expected behavior is no errors.

Related (maybe) issue: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/4924 (see `Test 3` output)

**Example:**

```systemverilog
module top;

  class my_class#(type t = bit);
  endclass

  my_class#(my_class#(bit)) mc;

endmodule
```

**Output:**

```text
%Error: top.sv:6:13: Duplicate parameter connection: '__paramNumber1'
    6 |   my_class#(my_class#(bit)) mc;
      |             ^~~~~~~~
        top.sv:6:23: ... Location of original parameter connection
    6 |   my_class#(my_class#(bit)) mc;
      |                       ^~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

**Command:**

```bash
verilator --binary top.sv
```

---

**Bypass:**

The problem can be solved by splitting the type declaration into parts using `typedef`:

```systemverilog
module top;

  class my_class#(type t = bit);
  endclass

  typedef my_class#(bit) mc_type_part;

  my_class#(mc_type_part) mc;

endmodule
```

---

Verilator version (git master): `Verilator 5.029 devel rev v5.028-88-g195a66529`

OS (verilator devcontainer): Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
